title: angelica cartilaginomarginata var. makinoi
intro: Angelica cartilaginomarginata var. makinoi, also known as Japanese angelica or Japanese wild angelica, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to reduce pain and swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Angelica cartilaginomarginata var. makinoi can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
